.. _tutorials:

Tutorials
=========

This page contains tutorials for using Ammonyte to detect abrupt transitions and tipping points in paleoclimate time series.

.. grid:: 3
   :gutter: 2
   :margin: 2

   .. card::
      :link: https://linked.earth/AmmonyteTutorials/Getting_Started.html
      :text-align: left

      **Getting Started with Ammonyte**

      Learn how to load a time series, explore its metadata, and create your first visualization using the ``Series`` class.

Getting Started with Detection Methods
"""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""""

The following tutorials demonstrate the three transition detection methods available in Ammonyte, all applied to the NGRIP ice core dataset.

.. grid:: 3
   :gutter: 2
   :margin: 2

   .. card::
      :link: https://linked.earth/AmmonyteTutorials/KS_test.html
      :text-align: left

      **Detecting Transitions with the KS Test**

      Detect abrupt climate transitions using the augmented Kolmogorov-Smirnov test based on the method of Bagniewski et al. (2021).

   .. card::
      :link: https://linked.earth/AmmonyteTutorials/LERM_validation.html
      :text-align: left

      **Detecting Transitions with LERM**

      Detect transitions using Laplacian Eigenmaps of Recurrence Matrices (LERM) and Fisher Information (James et al., 2024).

   .. card::
      :link: https://linked.earth/AmmonyteTutorials/Rupture_Validation.html
      :text-align: left

      **Detecting Transitions with Ruptures**

      Detect transitions using change-point detection algorithms, with validation against known Dansgaard-Oeschger events.
